name: Contecnica: Landing platform
description: Production monorepo with FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Next.js App Router—event-driven cache coherence, typed APIs, and operations-first packaging for a robust public site.
longDescription: Contecnica’s marketing stack is built as a production-oriented monorepo: an async FastAPI backend (SQLAlchemy 2, PostgreSQL, Redis, Alembic) and a Next.js App Router frontend (TypeScript, shadcn/ui, Motion). Content changes are written through the API first; the system broadcasts typed WebSocket invalidation so the public app re-fetches authoritative JSON—avoiding stale static bundles without coupling the editor to the edge cache implementation. Alembic migrations keep schema evolution traceable; Redis backs the fan-out channel with a narrow responsibility. Docker/Podman workflows, env documentation, and release bundles (including GHCR-oriented packaging) support repeatable deploys and reduce configuration drift.
features: REST API with async SQLAlchemy and migrations via Alembic—schema changes are versioned, not hand-edited
Next.js public site with server/client boundaries and typed content refetch after invalidation
Redis-backed notification path for content-change fan-out
WebSocket channel with origin checks and production TLS guidance
Docker Compose for local and production-shaped stacks
Release packaging scripts for on-server build and GHCR image pulls
Windows manage.ps1 / Node TUI for common operations
highlights: Single source of truth in PostgreSQL with explicit, event-driven cache invalidation
Architecture resists stale content: DB-first writes, Redis fan-out, client refetch on typed signals
Clear split between public Next.js surface and FastAPI admin/documentation concerns
Operational scripts and GHCR-oriented bundles aligned with container-first hosting
challenges: Marketing sites that mix CMS-like workflows with static performance need consistent cache semantics and safe realtime updates without stale public JSON.
solutions: Use DB-first writes, Redis for fan-out, and a client that refetches on invalidation while keeping production WebSocket and origin configuration documented next to compose files.
security: Origin-scoped WebSocket usage documented for dev vs production
Separate public vs admin concerns in API design—smaller blast radius for credentials
Secrets live only on deployment targets; source control carries env templates without live values
TLS and proxy configuration documented alongside compose so encrypted transport is the default posture
